data.notes: Not a real stall — the transcript shows the worker actively finishing legitimate work (43/43 tests green, DIAGNOSIS appended, SUMMARY mid-write) when the 15m marker window expired; it simply never reached the TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer. Retry on the same assignment and worktree: tell the worker the implementation and verification are already done and committed state is nearly complete — it should skip re-implementation, verify the existing worktree state (SUMMARY written, tests green), finish/commit the SUMMARY and DIAGNOSIS edits, and immediately emit the TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer as its very first priority rather than re-running the full verification block.

data.notes: The worker is not actually stuck — the transcript tail shows sound, active analysis: it correctly identified two false negatives in the plan's automated verify block (BSD `grep -rc file` prints `file:count` so the `= "1"` comparison can never pass, and the `status: "done"` grep matches pre-existing completion-status lines that are not scope exemptions). Its judgment to satisfy criterion INTENT without touching unrelated pre-existing code is correct. It simply overran the 15m window deliberating. Retry same assignment with this feedback: your analysis of the two verify false-negatives is correct and accepted — do NOT modify pre-existing status lines or remove the picomatch call; note both quirks as deviations in your summary, finish remaining work immediately, and emit the TICKMARKR_RESULT trailer without further deliberation. Time-box: commit and emit the trailer within the first 10 minutes.

data.summary: HARD-09 closed: literal basename-suffix fallback in assertWriteScope lets archived v1.11 dirs 35/36 compile clean while v1.9-29 (P30-02) still rejects, with all threemutation drills committed as non-blunting proof.
deviations: Plan <verify> grep -rc 'picomatch(' uses BSD 'file:count' format so the ='1' comparison never matches on macOS — INTENT satisfied (grep -c confirmsexactly 1 call), not patched in-place per feedback

Plan <verify> 'status...done' grep matches pre-existing task-status plumbing in gsd.ts (~line 140, predates this task) that is NOT a scope exemption — INTENTsatisfied (assertWriteScope keys off bare/files, never status); pre-existing status lines left untouched and picomatch call kept, per feedback

data.details:
3 commit(s):
.../41-scope-seam-integrity/41-02-DIAGNOSIS.md     | 132 +++++++++++++++++++++
 .../41-scope-seam-integrity/41-02-SUMMARY.md       |  73 ++++++++++++
 .../phases/41-scope-seam-integrity/41-RESEARCH.md  |  10 +-
 src/compile/common.ts                              |  34 ++++--
 src/compile/gsd.ts                                 |   8 +-
 tests/compile/scope-seam.test.ts                   |  32 +++++
 .../fixtures/scope-seam/defective-bare/01-PLAN.md  |  33 ++++++
 .../scope-seam/relocated-selfwrite/01-PLAN.md      |  33 ++++++
 8 files changed, 339 insertions(+), 16 deletions(-)

✓ Stale-by-relocation over-fire reproduced RED on real archived corpus with failure output committed, and the two vendored traps (must-keep-throwing, must-become-clean) in place: 41-02-DIAGNOSIS.md commits both the tsx probe output (both v1.11 dirs THROW) and the unedited vitest failure tail against unfixed HEAD f8d5b24; the diff adds both vendored traps: tests/fixtures/scope-seam/defective-bare (must keep throwing, pinned by the new HARD-09 test) and tests/fixtures/scope-seam/relocated-selfwrite (must become clean, added to LEGITIMATE).
✓ Relocation over-fire fixed by a literal, mutation-proven basename fallback: archived v1.11 corpus compiles clean, P30-02 shape still rejects, both drills' redness committed — guard demonstrably did not blunt the check: common.ts adds a literal `f === bare || f.endsWith(`/${bare}`)` fallback (no second matcher); both v1.11 dirs and relocated-selfwrite are in LEGITIMATE (suite green claimed with 798 tests); DEFECTIVE v1.9-29 pin unchanged and passing; drills A1/A2/B with observed vitest output are committed in the diagnosis, A1 showing the DEFECTIVE pin reddening when the guard is dropped.
✓ RED reproduced against unfixed HEAD using the REAL archived corpus (35-* and 36-* throw), literal vitest failure output from adding them to LEGITIMATE committed in 41-02-DIAGNOSIS.md before any src change (SC-3): Diagnosis pins the unfixed HEAD SHA f8d5b242…, shows both archived dirs throwing CompileError via the probe, and commits the unedited vitest tail (1 failed | 2 passed) from the LEGITIMATE additions — the failing test is the legitimate-corpus test, with stack frames at common.ts:40 / gsd.ts:127.
✓ After the fix, both archived v1.11 dirs compile clean via a relocation-invariant bare-name fallback — a literal basename-suffix test, deliberately NOT a second picomatch matcher: The fallback is a plain equality/endsWith string test in common.ts with an in-source comment naming the one-matcher decision; gsd.ts carries the bare name through instead of relying on the live-dir join; both dirs are added to LEGITIMATE with counts 1 and 2 and the suite is reported green.
✓ P30-02 shape STILL rejected: v1.9-29 throws, DEFECTIVE pin unchanged, committed mutation drill A1 (bare && guard dropped) shows the DEFECTIVE pin reddening (SC-4): The DEFECTIVE constant and its test are byte-unchanged in the diff; Drill A1's observed output (2 failed: v1.9-29 pin AND defective-bare guard) is committed in the diagnosis, proving the guard is load-bearing for the P30-02 rejection.
✓ Stronger non-blunting fact stated and verified: v1.9-29 structurally immune to any bare-gated loosening (all four directives full slashed paths, bare never set); Drill A2 reddens ONLY defective-bare while v1.9-29 correctly stays green: Diagnosis has a dedicated structural-immunity section with the grep of all pathish directives (four full slashed Create paths), and Drill A2's observed output shows exactly 1 failed (defective-bare) with v1.9-29 green, explicitly framed as correct and tied to the D38-G fabrication shape.
✓ Fallback is basename-precise, mutation-proven: committed drills show the defective-bare trap reddening under BOTH the bare-only collapse (A2) and substring containment (B): Drills A2 and B both show observed output with the defective-bare test failing; the fixture seeds the near-miss entry 99-01-SUMMARY.md that only a loose includes() would excuse, making Drill B non-vacuous.
✓ 41-RESEARCH.md's three factual errata corrected at source (v1.9-29 directive misquote ~line 250, oracle 6 dir-36 count 2 not 1, A1 setup ordering daemon.ts:215/217/218) and both Open Questions gain (RESOLVED): The diff edits 41-RESEARCH.md in place: ERRATUM 1 at the ~line-250 misquote (full slashed path, structurally immune), ERRATUM 2 fixing oracle 6's count to 2, ERRATUM 3 reversing the A1 setup-ordering claim with the daemon.ts:215/217/218 citation and the npm-install-no-op explanation, and both Open Questions carry (RESOLVED, 2026-07-11 P41-01) markers.
✓ No status:done exemption anywhere in src/compile/; no matcher option changed; picomatch options at common.ts:36 stay literal '{ dot: true }' with the byte-identity comment citing src/gates/scope.ts: The diff introduces no status-based logic; the `picomatch(files, { dot: true }); // byte-identical options to src/gates/scope.ts:15` line is unchanged in the diff context, and the fallback is a string test, not a matcher option change; SUMMARY additionally records exactly one picomatch( call.
✓ New corpus fixtures point ONLY at archived .planning/milestones/ dirs or vendored tests/fixtures/ dirs — never at live .planning/phases/ paths: LEGITIMATE additions are two .planning/milestones/v1.11-phases/ dirs and two tests/fixtures/scope-seam/ dirs; the relocated-selfwrite fixture's stale literal deliberately points at an archived-style .planning/milestones/v1.0-phases/ path, with an in-fixture comment naming the HYG-06 class.
✓ src/gates/, src/adapters/, src/config/ and package.json untouched; runtime deps remain exactly picomatch, yaml, zod: The diff touches only .planning/ docs, src/compile/common.ts, src/compile/gsd.ts, tests/compile/scope-seam.test.ts, and two vendored fixtures — no gates/adapters/config/package.json changes and no dependency changes.

data.summary: HARD-08 closed: scope gate fail-closed on worker deviations with operator allowlist-only relaxation, corpus-replay oracle pins 7 flips/2 recoveries, 803 testsgreen.

data.details:
2 commit(s):
.../41-scope-seam-integrity/41-01-DIAGNOSIS.md     | 115 +++++++++++++++++++++
 .../41-scope-seam-integrity/41-01-SUMMARY.md       |  44 ++++++++
 scripts/scan-scope-corpus.mjs                      | 109 +++++++++++++++++++
 src/adapters/prompt.ts                             |   2 +-
 src/config/config.ts                               |   7 ++
 src/gates/run-gates.ts                             |   2 +-
 src/gates/scope.ts                                 |  29 ++++--
 tests/fixtures/scope-corpus/replay.json            |  93 +++++++++++++++++
 tests/gates/evidence-scope.test.ts                 |  55 +++++++++-
 tests/run/daemon.test.ts                           |  16 +++
 10 files changed, 457 insertions(+), 15 deletions(-)

✓ HARD-08 fail-open reproduced RED against unfixed HEAD with failure output committed; corpus numbers machine-re-derived with provenance and vendored as replayable fixture: 41-01-DIAGNOSIS.md pins unfixed HEAD sha 1afb40c, gives the exact vitest command, and commits the literal failure output (3 scope-gate assertions plus the daemon oracle failing RED). Corpus numbers are re-derived by scripts/scan-scope-corpus.mjs with the run-dir provenance and stderr aggregates recorded, and tests/fixtures/scope-corpus/replay.json vendors the 7 fail-open entries as a replayable fixture.
✓ Design B+C is live: deviations are audit notes, operator allowlist is the only relaxation, prompt states the enforced rule, corpus numbers machine-checked by a replay oracle against the vendored fixture: scope.ts removes the deviations-length pass branch; deviations now only appear as a 'worker-declared deviations (audit note, not authority)' string in details. dispositionOffenders(offenders, allowDeviations) is the sole relaxation path, wired from run-gates.ts via ctx.cfg.scope?.allowDeviations ?? []. prompt.ts states the enforced rule. The 'HARD-08: corpus replay' test machine-checks 7 flips under [] and 2 recoveries under ['.planning/**'] against replay.json using the exported dispositionOffenders.
✓ Out-of-scope edit + ANY declared deviation FAILS — reproduced RED first, literal vitest output committed under a heading containing 'RED proof' (SC-1, D-04): The '## RED proof (HARD-08)' heading contains the exact string 'RED proof' and holds the literal vitest failure output. Tests 'liar worker' (deviation naming no path) and the reworked 'declared deviation does not pass' oracle both assert pass === false; the daemon-level test (T1 edits README.md out of scope, declares it, expects s.done === []) proves the runtime wiring, and all appear failing in the committed RED output.
✓ NAMING IS NOT SUFFICIENT: a deviation naming the offending path verbatim still fails under default config, red on unfixed HEAD: Test 'HARD-08: naming the path is not sufficient' declares deviations ['README.md — needed a usage note'] naming the offending path and asserts pass === false with no allowlist; the committed RED output shows this exact test failing ('expected true to be false') against unfixed HEAD, distinguishing B+C from a string-matching design A.
✓ Out-of-scope edit passes ONLY when path matches scope.allowDeviations globs; an allowlist naming a DIFFERENT path does not grant it: Test 'HARD-08: operator allowlist is the only relaxation' asserts pass with allowlist ['README.md'] and fail with allowlist ['package-lock.json'] for the same README.md offender — no blanket grant. dispositionOffenders puts non-matching offenders into hard[], which fails the gate.
✓ Corpus numbers provenance-pinned by committed re-runnable scripts/scan-scope-corpus.mjs regenerating replay.json byte-for-byte, each entry carrying the gate-result details string and source journal line index (fields absent from 41-RESEARCH.md): scripts/scan-scope-corpus.mjs is committed, zero-dep, deterministic (sorted entries, stable JSON.stringify), and DIAGNOSIS records the `diff - replay.json && echo REPRODUCIBLE` byte-identity check. Every replay.json entry carries `details` (the verbatim gate-result string) and `line` (journal line index) — transcription-detection fields a research-table paste could not supply.
✓ prompt.ts no longer teaches the blanket escape hatch; instruction states the enforced rule: The 'If you must edit outside the file scope, do it, but declare each such path in deviations' line is replaced with wording stating out-of-scope edits FAIL the scope gate unless operator-allowlisted, that declaring a deviation never passes the gate, and that deviations are journaled for the operator's audit — the enforced B+C rule.
✓ One matcher semantics: { dot: true } unchanged at both sites, allowlist matcher uses same options, scope.ts gains reciprocal comment referencing assertWriteScope by symbol not line: src/compile/common.ts is untouched by the diff; scope.ts's inScope matcher keeps picomatch(files, { dot: true }) unchanged and gains the comment '// byte-identical options to assertWriteScope in src/compile/common.ts' — symbol reference, no line number. The new allowlist matcher in dispositionOffenders uses picomatch(allowDeviations, { dot: true }), the same options.
✓ src/compile/common.ts, src/compile/gsd.ts and package.json untouched; runtime deps remain picomatch, yaml, zod: None of the three files appear in the diff. No dependency changes anywhere; scan script uses only node:fs/node:path/node:url built-ins, and the gate change reuses the already-installed picomatch.

reviewer claude-code:fable (anthropic): approved
- dispositionOffenders + gate rewrite correctly implement B+C: deviations are echoed only as an audit note, allowlist is the sole pass authority, and {dot:true} is preserved at both matcher sites — no correctness bugs found in the enforcement path.
- Minor: the corpus-replay test's final loop `replay.filter((x) => !x.historicalPass)` is vacuous — replay.json contains only historicalPass:true entries, so it asserts nothing today; harmless but gives false coverage comfort.
- Minor: scripts/scan-scope-corpus.mjs parseOffenders drops operator-allowlisted paths from the fail branch (hard-only list), so a future mixed allowed+hard entry would under-record offenders; and deviation strings containing '; '/newlines would confuse the 'declared:' line parsing. Corpus-scoped, acceptable.
- Note: byte-for-byte regeneration is only reproducible on the operator machine (.tickmarkr/runs is uncommitted and still growing — diagnosis already shows 132 vs research's 125 events); the vendored fixture keeps CI stable, but a future rescan after more runs may legitimately change the pinned counts 7/2. This matches the acceptance criteria as written, just flagging the maintenance edge.
- Minor: the pass-with-allowlist details line uses the exact prefix 'out-of-scope but operator-allowlisted:' which the scan script already parses — good forward consistency; but note run-gates passes `ctx.cfg.scope?.allowDeviations ?? []`, so a config with `scope: {}` (partial) behaves as fail-closed default, as intended.
